original_hypothesis.title: SignalService is intentionally unpausable; any discovered proof-verification bug becomes non-containable at contract level
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: SignalService inherits pause/unpause but overrides _authorizePause to always revert, making pause/unpause unusable. If a critical vulnerability is discovered in proof verification (LibTrieProof / MerkleTrie / RLP parsing), the protocol cannot freeze SignalService to halt proof acceptance. Bridge/vaults may be pausable, but any other consumers (now or future) may remain exploitable, and proofs may still be cached on-chain by proveSignalReceived side effects.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operational containment risk rather than an immediate exploit. Given SignalService sits at the trust boundary for all bridging proofs, inability to pause increases incident response difficulty.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function _authorizePause(address) internal pure override { revert SS_UNSUPPORTED(); }

preconditions: A vulnerability exists in SignalService proof verification or related libraries
Attack can be executed without needing SignalService to be paused (likely)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No on-chain kill switch for the proof verification layer
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: During any incident affecting SignalService correctness

operational_questions: What is the emergency response plan if SignalService proof logic is found vulnerable?
Can pausing Bridge/vaults alone fully contain impact, considering caching side effects in proveSignalReceived?
Are there any other contracts relying on SignalService that cannot be paused?
validation_requirements: Map all SignalService consumers and their pause controls
Simulate incident response: pause Bridge/vaults and assess whether harmful caching is still possible
Review upgrade paths and timelock delays for emergency fixes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Exploit a hypothetical proof verification bug
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Continue exploiting even after Bridge/vaults paused by targeting other SignalService consumers or caching attack surfaces
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Force protocol to rely on upgrades-only response

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Incident containment delay; extended exploit window
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High under active exploit; requires upgrade execution under governance
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All proof-based cross-chain verification relying on SignalService

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Unusual proveSignalReceived call patterns and ChainDataSynced caching spikes
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Attackers probing RLP/proof parsing edge cases

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
